evals.prompt: Tests are failing on the login page. The error says 'expected redirect to /dashboard, got /login'. I tried fixing it twice already — once by clearing cookies, once by restarting the server. Both didn't help. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as Failure Mode 1 (specific bug, first/second attempt) and runs the 4-phase root cause discipline: read the error carefully, reproduce, hypothesize root cause distinct from symptom, verify before fixing. Does NOT just guess another fix attempt.
expectations: Classifies this as Failure Mode 1 (specific bug, isolated)
Does NOT immediately suggest another fix
Reads the error carefully and notes the redirect mismatch
Asks for or seeks to reproduce the issue (smallest reproduction)
Hypothesizes a root cause distinct from the symptom
States the root cause clearly before proposing any fix

evals.prompt: I've been trying to add a feature for 3 hours. Each fix breaks something else. There are now 6 failing tests, the auth middleware is patched in 4 places, and I can't remember what the original feature was supposed to do. Help.
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as Failure Mode 2 (session has gone wrong) and proposes a hard reset: save a reset note, end session, start fresh with `/remember restore` and the reset note as context.
expectations: Classifies this as Failure Mode 2 (session has gone wrong, multiple failed fixes)
Does NOT suggest another fix attempt
Proposes a hard reset (end session, save reset note, start fresh)
Mentions writing a reset note capturing what went wrong
Mentions using remember restore to recover prior state if available
Does NOT try to continue patching in the current session

evals.prompt: I asked you to use OAuth for login and you went and built a custom JWT session system from scratch. It works but it's not what I asked for and it doesn't integrate with our existing auth provider. How do we recover?
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as Failure Mode 3 (foundation is wrong — wrong assumption about what was requested) and proposes a rethink: name the wrong assumption, propose correct approach, do not start rebuilding until developer confirms.
expectations: Classifies this as Failure Mode 3 (foundation is wrong — wrong assumption about requirements)
Names the wrong assumption (OAuth was requested, JWT was built)
Does NOT suggest patching the JWT system to be more OAuth-like
Proposes a rethink with the correct approach (use OAuth, integrate with existing auth)
Distinguishes what can be salvaged from what must be discarded
Waits for developer confirmation before any rebuilding
